# SC-20: Runtime Anomaly Detection

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** COULD (optional) · **Status:** draft

Detect behavioural anomalies through statistical baseline monitoring: identify deviations in system behaviour patterns, detect AI model drift, identify adversarial input patterns, and surface any runtime behaviour inconsistent with the approved design. Feeds detected anomalies into the SC-19 incident process when thresholds are exceeded.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 6 | Observability & Maintenance | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Automated with human escalation |
| **Agent does** | Maintain behavioural baselines; apply statistical anomaly detection; detect AI model drift and adversarial inputs; escalate on anomaly. |
| **Human does** | Investigate escalated anomalies; determine whether anomaly represents a security incident or requires code change. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Anomaly detection record | log_entry | Continuous record of detected behavioural anomalies with severity and baseline deviation measurements. | 7 years (DORA) |
| Anomaly escalation alert | alert | Alert when anomaly exceeds defined threshold; includes context for human investigation. |  |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | continuous |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | escalate |
| **Escalate to** | Security Architect |

High-severity anomalies trigger immediate escalation to Security Architect and may initiate feedback loop.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 10 | Detection of anomalous activities and ICT-related incidents through continuous monitoring |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(3) | Resilience of AI systems against errors, inconsistencies, and adversarial inputs |
| EU AI Act | Art. 9(2)(b) | Continuous monitoring of identified risks during operation |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security / SRE · **Owner role:** Security Architect
